Off Calendar Dec-05-2022 Continued To Dec-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elisabeth Hanowsky, via Zoom, for Stephen Ernst and Kristina Edwards, Personal Representatives.

The court confirms with counsel of the plan to obtain waivers and to file a declaration regarding receipts soon and proceed via the previously mentioned waivers.

The court takes the Status of Interim Accounting off calendar and continues the Status on Close of Estate to Dec-19-2022 to allow for the processing of the Status Petition that is pending in the court's e-filing queue.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-01-2022 Continued To Nov-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elisabeth Hanowsky, via Zoom, for Stephen Ernst and Kristina Edwards, Personal Representatives.

The court confirms that there are no appearances to object to this matter and advises of its intent to take this matter under submission with the intent to strike the portion from the proposed order submitted to the court regarding direction to First American Title Company. Counsel does not object to the court's tentative ruling and advises that there has been a change to the proposed order and advises that she intends to resubmit an updated order via the court's e-filing system accordingly. The court queries counsel regarding whether she may be able to obtain waivers of accounting. Counsel advises that she intends to confirm the preceding with the two beneficiaries. The court confirms that if an accounting is required, it should cover two years forward from the date of death.

The Petition for Order for Preliminary Distribution, etc., is taken under submission. The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Nov-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of Interim Accounting is added for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elisabeth Hanowsky appeared by zoom for Kristina Edwards and Stephen Ernst (Petitioners).

The Court stated that it reviewed the file and it seems to be in order. Ms. Hanowsky stated that she has nothing to add, spoke of Judge Moody's concerns at the last hearing and stated that she filed the report of the Investigator showing that the last known forwarding address for Ms. Mansfieled is the Daughter's address. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated September 6, 2013 is admitted to probate. Stephen Ernst and Kristina Edwards are appointed as Co-Executors of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed. Status on close of estate is set for July 11, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Continued To Jan-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elisabeth Hanowsky appeared by zoom for Christina Edwards and Stephen Ernst (Petitioners).

The Court stated that the issue is whether sufficient evidence was provided to overcome the presumption of revocation and whether enough has been done to search for and locate Cynthia Mansfield. Ms. Hanowsky stated that the prior Attorney interviewed the siblings, that the siblings had not spoken with Cynthia in over 20-years and they refused to provide them with Cyntia's birthdate. Ms. Hanowsky also stated that they did find out that Cynthia was a real estate agent in San Diego, that notice was mailed to the San Diego Department of Real Estate and that the real estate department did not know if the address that was provided is accurate. In addition, Ms. Hanowsky stated that they served notice to anyone they located with that name with address listed since 2006, that they sent notice to six different addresses, that they did publication just a few weeks ago on October 29th and that publication was done three times. Furthermore, Ms. Hanowsky stated that based on the mailed notices, communication with the family and publication, they have made good and best efforts to locate Cynthia. The Court stated that with an estate of this size, it feels that this requires internet searches, that there hasn't been enough time since notice went out and that it will continue this for Ms. Hanowsky to take steps to locate Ms. Mansfield. Ms. Hanowsky stated tht the prior Attorney did an ancestry.com search. The Court raised the issue of engaging the services of a Private Investigator with access to do a superior data base. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to January 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Continued To Nov-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-16-2020 Continued To Oct-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-08-2020 Continued To Sep-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Megan Holbrook appeared by telephone for Stephen Ernst and Kristina Edwards.

The Court asks if the will was ever found. Ms. Holbrook stated that the will was not found, that it took over six months to access the safe deposit box, that the will was not there and requested a 60-day continuance to provide evidence to rebut presumption of revocation. Ms. Holbrook also stated that there are witnesses, that declarations can be filed and that she thinks they filed amended documents. In addition, Ms. Holbrook stated that they located other persons who are entitled to notice and that they sent them notice. The Court noted that page 2 #3.d. annual gross income in the petition is b blank. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to September 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
